title: Hoya lii
intro: Hoya lii, also known as the waxflower or porcelain flower, is a flowering plant species in the family Apocynaceae. Traditionally used in folk medicine, Hoya lii has been reported to possess various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory disorders. The bioactive constituents of Hoya lii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hoya lii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into formulations for anxiety relief and sleep aids.
taxonomy: Hoya lii is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to Thailand and is commonly known as the Waxflower or Porcelain Flower. The Hoya lii is part of the genus Hoya, which consists of over 500 species of evergreen climbing plants. Hoya lii is a popular ornamental plant due to its fragrant, waxy flowers and ability to thrive in a variety of environments. The species is often cultivated for its attractive white or pink blooms and ability to attract pollinators.
